sections.paragraph:
ἐκείνης adverbial gen. of ἐκεῖνος (sc. τῆς ὁδοῦ) there Lk 19:4 (here D has the st. dat. form ἐκείνῃ Hdt. 8, 106; Thu. 4, 77 et al.; s. B-D-F §186, 1; Rdm. 22 135, 2; Rob. 494).—WHavers, IndogF 19, 1906, 83ff. Frisk s.v. ἐκεῖνος. S. entry κἀκεῖνος.

gen. gen. = genitive

  1. sc. = scilicet (one may understand, supply)

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

dat. dat. = dative

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

Rdm. Rdm. = LRadermacher, Neutestamentliche Grammatik—List 6

Rob. Rob. = ATRobertson, A Grammar of the Greek NT in the Light of Historical Research—List 6

IndogF IndogF = Indogermanische Forschungen—List 6

Frisk Frisk = HF., Griechisches etymologisches Wörterbuch—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 301
